shorts.content: I realized at my 40th birthday that I couldn't breathe—and I had to make a change. Running the same restaurant for 19 years, being a mom, going through divorce... I was drowning in my own life. Sometimes the thing you built becomes the thing that's suffocating you.
shorts.title: The moment I knew everything had to change

shorts.content: Relationships are more important than education in business. I've seen people with all the credentials fail because they can't connect with people. Meanwhile, the most successful entrepreneurs I know? They invest in authentic relationships first, everything else second.
shorts.title: Why your MBA won't save your business

shorts.hook: COVID gave us permission to choose
shorts.content: COVID gave permission to choose differently—and that choice can lead to freedom. For the first time, we all had to stop and question: Is this working? Do I want to keep doing this? That pause became the catalyst for reinvention.
shorts.title: How 2020 changed everything for women leaders

shorts.content: I ran the same restaurant for 19 years while navigating motherhood and divorce. People think consistency means never changing, but real leadership means evolving your business as you evolve as a person. Your business should grow with you, not trap you.
shorts.title: What 19 years in restaurants taught me about leadership

titleOptions.curiosityGap: What Happens When You Can't Breathe in Your Own Business?
titleOptions.boldStatement: Why This Chef Chose Divorce Over Her 19-Year Restaurant Empire
titleOptions.numberBased: 5 Signs Your Business Success is Actually Suffocating You

description:
What happens when the business you built becomes the prison you're trapped in? Chef Lindsay Smith's raw story of running the same restaurant for 19 years will change how you think about success.

From burnout at 40 to breakthrough reinvention, this conversation reveals what it really takes to lead authentically in business and life.

⏰ TIMESTAMPS:
00:00 Introduction
03:15 The moment everything had to change
08:30 19 years, same restaurant, different woman
15:45 Navigating divorce while running a business
22:10 Why relationships matter more than education
28:40 From operator to owner mindset shift
35:20 Building authentic community in digital age
42:15 Perimenopause and permission to choose differently

🔥 KEY TAKEAWAYS:
• Your business should evolve as you evolve—pivot when life demands it
• Relationships are the foundation of long-term business success
• Learning to delegate creates space for growth and freedom
• Major life transitions can become catalysts for empowerment
• Middle age can bring unexpected clarity and confidence

🤝 CONNECT WITH LINDSAY:
📸 Instagram: @nirvana.laguna (https://www.instagram.com/nirvana.laguna/)
🔗 Website: https://nirvanlaguna.com

📖 FULL SHOW NOTES: https://themanageher.com/blog/chef-lindsay-smith-19-years-divorce-rebuilding

📊 TAKE THE QUIZ: https://themanageher.com/blog/chef-lindsay-smith-19-years-divorce-rebuilding

🔔 SUBSCRIBE for weekly leadership insights that actually work for ambitious women

#WomenLeadership #Entrepreneur #Burnout #BusinessPivot #RestaurantOwner #Resilience #WomenInBusiness #Leadership #Reinvention #AuthenticLeadership

communityPosts.content:
Real talk: What's your biggest leadership challenge right now? 🤔

After talking with Chef Lindsay Smith about her 19-year journey from burnout to breakthrough, I'm curious what's keeping you up at night as a leader.

A) Feeling trapped in the day-to-day operations
B) Struggling to delegate and trust others
C) Balancing business growth with personal life
D) Knowing when it's time to pivot or change direction

Drop a comment and tell me WHY you chose that option. Your struggles might be exactly what another woman leader needs to hear. 💪

"I realized at my 40th birthday that I couldn't breathe—and I had to make a change." - Chef Lindsay Smith 🍃

This hit me HARD in our latest podcast episode. How many of us are so busy building our businesses that we forget to check if we can actually breathe in them?

Lindsay ran the same restaurant for 19 years, navigated divorce, raised kids, and still found a way to reinvent herself completely. Her story is proof that it's never too late to choose differently.

Watch the full conversation: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SVLzi5IeDBM

What would you do if you gave yourself permission to breathe? 💨✨
